text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 30 April 2016 

Dr Helen Murray  
St. Bartholomew’s Hospital  
Department of Neurology, West Smithfield, London EC1A 7BE  

Dear Dr Helen Murray,  

Alex Turner, DOB: 14-03-1987, Hospital No: H382917 NHS No. 9034712568  
Flat 2, 18 Fleet Street, London, EC4Y 1AA 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures of uncertain aetiology (initial presentation 2012)  
Seizure-free off ASMs since 25 Jun 2015 

Current Medication: 

None (antiseizure medications previously discontinued June 2015) 

Plan: 

• Maintain seizure diary and agreed safety measures  
• No routine neurology follow-up; patient to contact clinic if any recurrence or concerns  
• Written advice provided regarding driving regulations, sleep hygiene, alcohol moderation, and managing medications across two households 

I reviewed Alex Turner in clinic today. They report that they split their time between two households each week and have established a practical routine of keeping necessary medications and personal health items at both locations to avoid missed doses when required. They are currently not taking any antiseizure medication, having tapered off in mid-2015 under prior supervision, and remain well with no events. Specifically, they confirm they have been Seizure-free off ASMs since 25 Jun 2015. There have been no episodes of loss of awareness, unexplained collapses, myoclonic jerks, or nocturnal events reported by bed partners. 

We discussed general risk reduction, including adequate sleep, avoiding binge alcohol use, and prompt review if any warning symptoms occur. Given the stability since 2015 and absence of red flags, I have not arranged a routine follow-up, but I would be happy to review sooner if symptoms recur or circumstances change (for example, new medications, intercurrent illness, or pregnancy planning). Written information has been provided detailing what to do in the event of a suspected breakthrough event, and the patient is aware of DVLA guidance should any seizures recur. 

With best wishes,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the patient has been seizure-free off antiseizure medications since 25 Jun 2015. The clinic date is 30 April 2016. Calculate duration seizure-free: from 25 Jun 2015 to 25 Apr 2016 is 10 months; plus 5 additional days to 30 Apr 2016 does not change the month count. Therefore, the patient has been seizure-free for 10 months, which meets the ≥6 months criterion for 'seizure free'. No other seizure frequencies are provided. Hence normalize to 'seizure free for 10 month'.
